summary: Phase 29 Weekly War Room — end-to-end test. BACKEND: 18/19 pytest pass. One FAILURE caught a real backend bug — the followup_pending bucket classification can never fire because compute_lead_health() does not return an 'overdue_count' field. All other rules (recently_won, blocked, inactive, commercial_pending) verified with synthetic leads. Sessions API fully working: start/active/notes PATCH/discuss/end (idempotent) + materialized_task_count + RBAC. Gemini AI summary call succeeded (confirmed in Past Reviews UI: '2 tasks created' + executive_summary text). Customer 403 correct on both /board and /sessions/*. Regression smoke (auth/leads/partner-intelligence/predictive-forecast) green. FRONTEND: All 7 buckets render with correct testids, 47 lead cards loaded, 5 KPI cards visible (Open Leads / Pipeline / Weighted / At Risk / Inactive), review-mode flow works (banner+LIVE badge, notes textarea, click-to-discuss with opacity-60 + active-lead-context sidebar), debounced notes save, End Review → AI completed → Past Reviews shows the just-ended session with 'N tasks created' + executive_summary preview. Help page has Weekly War Room section. Sidebar nav present. Customer redirected away from /war-room (correctly gated).

critical.issue: followup_pending bucket rule never fires because compute_lead_health() does not expose 'overdue_count' in its returned dict. Classifier reads health.get('overdue_count') which is always None/0. Also affects the lead card payload field 'overdue_count' which is always None on the board. FIX: in compute_lead_health (server.py ~line 3590-3601), after computing 'overdue' add it to the returned dict as 'overdue_count': overdue.

minor.issue: high_priority rule requires health_band in ('hot','at_risk') AND deal_value>=100k AND days_inactive<=14. Synthetic newly-created leads with no follow-up/comment history score 50 → band='cold' or 'warm', so high_priority is hard to trigger via fresh seed data. In production the 47 fixture leads happen to be in 'hot' band so the bucket does populate (verified in UI: 16 leads in High Priority). Rule is functional but very narrow.

Customer is not explicitly 403'd on list endpoint — function only blocks customer for board/start/active but list-sessions/get-session allow customer (just returns empty/owned). Tests show 200 with empty array. Consider explicit 403 for parity.
Gemini model id 'gemini-3.1-pro-preview' is hard-coded — verify alignment with EMERGENT_LLM_KEY routing; AI completed successfully in test, so no functional bug, just brittle.

integration_issues.issue: After clicking End Review and AI returns, the [data-testid='summary-dialog'] sometimes does not become visible within 45s in the same tab even though the underlying POST /end succeeded (verified: session ended, 2 tasks created, executive_summary persisted, Past Reviews sheet shows the session). May be a Radix Dialog mount race with the long-running async POST OR setSummaryOpen runs before currentSession is set. Manually opening from Past Reviews works fine. LOW priority — user can still see summary via History sheet.

action_items: P0 BACKEND BUG: In /app/backend/server.py compute_lead_health() (~line 3620-3625) add 'overdue_count': overdue to the returned dict. Without this fix, the War Room 'Follow-up Pending' bucket can never populate and lead-card overdue_count is always null. Reproduce: seed a lead with one past-dated unfinished follow_up — it lands in 'inactive' or is dropped instead of 'followup_pending'.

P2 (nice-to-have): Frontend WarRoom.jsx endSession() — instead of relying on setSummaryOpen(true) right after the await, also set window.__currentSummary = result so the SummaryDialog has stable data on first render, and avoid the dialog race.
P3: Add explicit 403 for customer on GET /api/war-room/sessions and GET /api/war-room/sessions/{id} (currently allows pass-through with empty/owned results).
critical_code_review_comments: BUG: server.py compute_lead_health() returns dict with score/band/days_inactive/factors — NO overdue_count. War Room classifier (line 5687) depends on it. Two consumers in server.py also reference h.get('overdue_count') (e.g. lines 4630, 5844 card payload) — all silently get 0/None.

WarRoom.jsx endSession uses native window.confirm — fine for desktop testing but not mobile-friendly and not auto-acceptable in Playwright unless dialog handler is registered. Consider an AlertDialog (already in shadcn) for consistency.

_classify_war_room_bucket priority order is sound (won → blocked → inactive → high_priority → followup_pending → commercial_pending → partner_coordination). However high_priority's band==hot/at_risk gate is so narrow that the bucket effectively requires existing engagement history; new leads will skip directly into commercial_pending/partner_coordination/None.
Gemini call wraps emergentintegrations and handles JSON parse failure gracefully (fallback executive_summary=raw[:500]). Good defensive coding.
Materialized Task creation correctly persists source='war_room_session' + source_session_id for traceability. Notification on cross-user assignment is best-effort (try/except) — fine.
Auto-close prior session logic in /sessions/start is correct and idempotent — verified by test_session_start_auto_closes_prior.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_phase29_war_room.py (NEW — 19 pytest cases: board shape/fields/customer-403, 5 classification rules with synthetic leads, sessions start/auto-close/active/notes/discuss/end/idempotent/list/detail/customer-403, materialized-tasks marker, 4 regression smoke)

success_rate.backend: 94.7% — 18/19 pytest pass; 1 fail caught real backend bug (overdue_count missing from compute_lead_health)
success_rate.frontend: 92% — 11/12 flows pass; only end-review summary-dialog auto-open has a Radix mount race, but end-of-session + AI summary + materialized tasks all confirmed via Past Reviews UI

test_credentials: admin@vyapaarnetwork.com/admin123, john@testco.com/test123 — both work. Auth returns 'access_token' (not 'token').
seed_data_creation: Synthetic leads prefixed TEST_WR_{recently_won,blocked,inactive,hp,fp,cp}_<uuid> inserted into db.leads then cleaned up via fixture teardown. Session 'TEST_WR Session 1' & 'TEST_WR Session 2' inserted into db.war_room_sessions then cleaned up. Tasks with source_session_id of test session cleaned up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 29 backend tests are at /app/backend/tests/test_phase29_war_room.py. After the main agent patches compute_lead_health() to expose overdue_count, re-run only TestBucketClassification — it will go from 1 fail to all pass. Authentication note: backend returns 'access_token' (not 'token') — already handled. Frontend tests can use page.on('dialog', accept) to bypass the native window.confirm in endSession. Past Reviews sheet is the source of truth that end-session completed even if summary-dialog has a mount race.
rca of the issue: RCA — followup_pending bucket bug: in server.py around lines 3589-3601 the function computes a local variable `overdue` (count of unfinished follow_ups whose scheduled_date < today) and uses it to adjust the health score, but the returned dict on line 3621-3626 only includes score/band/days_inactive/factors. Downstream in _classify_war_room_bucket (line 5687) and on the war-room card payload (line 5844), code reads health.get('overdue_count') which is permanently None. Reproduction: insert a lead with `{follow_ups: [{is_completed:false, scheduled_date: <past>}], deal_value:25000, status_id: <open status>, updated_at:<recent>}` → call GET /api/war-room/board → lead is filtered out by classifier's `days_inactive<14` fallback (returns None), never landing in followup_pending. Fix: one-line — add `'overdue_count': overdue,` to the returned dict at line ~3624. Initialize `overdue = 0` outside the follow_ups branch so it's always defined (currently scoped inside `if follow_ups:`).
